articles.title: TIM COOK SAYS APPLE WILL ‘BREAK NEW GROUND’ IN GENAI THIS YEAR
articles.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced that the company will make significant strides in General AI technology in the coming year. The company is refocusing its resources as it closes out its electric vehicle project. It plans to enhance products like Siri and iOS features with GenAI models, aiming for more sophisticated capabilities. Apple has been cautious in its GenAI developments, but it is starting to increase its presence in GenAI research and open-source contributions.

articles.title: 15+ IMPROVEMENTS AND NEW FEATURES COME TO FIGMA PROTOTYPING
articles.summary: Figma has introduced over 15 new improvements to enhance prototyping, including resizable device frames for mobile prototypes and various editing speed optimizations like simpler interaction copying and advanced overlay modes. The update enhances performance, leading to 22% fewer loading spinners in common scenarios.

articles.title: FORMER SNAP DESIGN LEAD DEBUTS SHADER, AN AR CREATION TOOL THAT USES AI TO GENERATE CUSTOM EFFECTS
articles.summary: Shader is disrupting the augmented reality scene with its user-friendly no-code AR creation tool. The platform, led by former Snap design lead Darya Sesitskaya, enables the quick creation of custom effects, leveraging AI to generate AR filters from text prompts. It currently offers a beta iOS app for real-time AR camera effects. Shader has raised $580,000 for further development. It is planning to implement premium features and expand into social media integration and voice-to-AR capabilities.

articles.title: HOW MY DREAM DESIGN JOB TURNED INTO A NIGHTMARE
articles.summary: Designers shouldn’t persist in dead-end jobs just because it’s easier to stay. They should see this time as a transitional career period, build up their portfolio, and leave for greener pastures.

articles.title: AI AND DESIGN SYSTEMS
articles.summary: This article looks at how AI can be applied to design systems. Use cases include writing component code, translating components across frameworks, writing unit tests, reviewing accessibility, writing documentation, and making documentation more accessible/inclusive.

articles.title: BEYOND NUMBERS: PROVING THE VALUE OF DESIGN(ERS)
UX COPYWRITING AND STYLE GUIDELINES
articles.summary: UX Language is a comprehensive set of UX copywriting and style guidelines, with examples to reference, for creating products.

articles.title: WRITE DOWN YOUR THOUGHTS RIGHT IN THE BROWSER (CHROME EXTENSION)
TURN ANY SELFIE INTO A CARTOON
articles.summary: Turn yourself or your loved ones into characters in a whimsical cartoon world.

articles.title: 2024 TECH LAYOFFS: DESIGNERS, I HAVE SOME GOOD NEWS
articles.summary: LinkedIn reported 1,490,000 design jobs in tech in October 2023. The US Bureau of Labor Statistics expects a 16% increase in digital designer jobs by 2032. NNG predicts that industry growth between now and 2050 will exceed previous records.

articles.title: HOW AI WILL INFLUENCE CREATIVE TOOLS: A CONVERSATION WITH REPLIT’S DAVID HOANG
articles.summary: Between the current job market and the things AI can automate, being a designer can feel scary. However, designers will always have a purpose and a role because there are always things that must be designed.

articles.title: APPLE'S NOTCH EXPLORATIONS SHOW WHAT WE COULD HAVE GOTTEN INSTEAD OF THE DYNAMIC ISLAND
articles.summary: A rare behind-the-scenes look at Apple's design process and what we may have gotten instead of the Dynamic Island. Various concepts for the Dynamic Island were considered, from the acceptable to the absurd. Some designs even proposed two notches. See how the notch evolved into the now ubiquitous Dynamic Island after six years of brainstorming.

articles.title: DESIGN IS NOT A SPECTATOR SPORT
articles.summary: ZDC is an IBM program that empowers designers, engineers, architects, and product managers to build user relationships.

articles.title: PROVOCATIVE BILLBOARD ADS GIVE IKEA A TASTE OF ITS OWN MEDICINE
articles.summary: Vintage furniture company Vinterior created the billboard ad mocking Ikea that has appeared alongside Ikea's posters at London Underground stations.
